notes: Realistic prompts a real user (Adir, owner of @personalfinancetips) would type. Mix of clear-intent and slightly-fuzzy phrasings in both English and Hebrew. Expected behaviour: skill triggers, runs the 6-step flow, writes a JSON file under output/ following references/output-schema.md.

evals.prompt: Make me 2 carousels about emergency funds for @personalfinancetips. Research YouTube first and follow the 7-slide framework.
evals.expected_output: Output JSON at output/emergency-*.json with schema_version=1, exactly 2 carousels, each with 7 slides in EN and 7 in HE. No dash characters in any Hebrew field. Each non-CTA slide has a ref_image from the 7-image pool. Both CTA slides' headlines end with @personalfinancetips.

evals.prompt: תכין לי קרוסלות על חיסכון חודשי. קח 3 סרטונים מיוטיוב, תקח את התמלול עם אפיפיי, ותוציא קרוסלה אחת באנגלית והעתק שלה בעברית טבעית. שים לב שאני לא רוצה לראות "-" בטקסט העברי.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on the Hebrew request. Runs pipeline, produces 1 carousel (not 2 — user specified 'קרוסלה אחת'). Hebrew reads native, no dashes, no AI-slop openers. Handles the explicit dash ban.

evals.prompt: I'm adding a topic dropdown to the client dashboard. I want the backend to call this skill for 'high-yield savings accounts' and get back JSON the frontend can render. Run it end-to-end and show me the file path + schema.
evals.expected_output: Uses headless path (run_pipeline.py) since the user framed it as a dashboard integration. Produces JSON, prints path, summarises the JSON contract. Does NOT scaffold any dashboard code.
